industries.name: NdFeB Magnet Grain Boundary Diffusion
uses: Terbium (with dysprosium) added to NdFeB sintered magnets significantly increases coercivity (resistance to demagnetization at elevated temperatures) — essential for EV traction motors and wind turbine generators where operating temperatures reach 150–180°C

Grain boundary diffusion (GBD) process: terbium fluoride or terbium oxide applied to magnet surface and diffused along grain boundaries at 800–900°C — achieves same coercivity enhancement as bulk substitution with 50–70% less Tb usage

Terbium content in high-coercivity NdFeB magnets for automotive applications: 0.5–2% Tb replacing or supplementing dysprosium, which is more abundant but less effective per unit weight at improving coercivity
Critical material designation: terbium is listed as a critical mineral by the US, EU, and Japan due to supply concentration in China (~99% of world production from ionic clay deposits in South China)

companies.name: Shin-Etsu Chemical
companies.use: World's largest NdFeB magnet producer and major consumer of terbium for high-coercivity magnet grades. Uses terbium in grain boundary diffusion and bulk substitution to produce EV motor magnets meeting high-temperature performance specifications.

companies.name: Vacuumschmelze (VAC)
companies.use: Major German NdFeB magnet manufacturer. Produces terbium-containing high-coercivity magnet grades for industrial servo motors, automotive applications, and wind turbine pitch control systems.

industries.name: Phosphors & Displays
uses: Terbium(III) green phosphor (LaPO₄:Ce,Tb; (Ce,Tb)MgAl₁₁O₁₉, CAT): the essential green component in fluorescent lamp tricolor phosphors — Tb³⁺ f-f emission at 543nm produces the saturated green that completes the RGB triad alongside Eu³⁺ red and Eu²⁺ blue
Terbium green phosphor in compact fluorescent lamps (CFLs) and T5/T8 fluorescent tubes for commercial and industrial lighting — every tricolor fluorescent lamp contains terbium
Tb³⁺-activated phosphors for white LED lighting: terbium contributes to green emission in some LED phosphor formulations for high-color-rendering applications

Tb-doped scintillator crystals and films for X-ray imaging: Tb-doped Gd₂O₂S (GOS) phosphor is the standard scintillator screen material in X-ray cassettes and digital radiography flat panels — Tb³⁺ provides high-efficiency green emission matching the sensitivity of CCD and CMOS imagers

companies.name: Kasei Optonix (Konica Minolta)
companies.use: Major X-ray scintillator and phosphor manufacturer. Produces Tb-doped gadolinium oxysulfide (GOS) scintillator screens and powders for digital radiography, CR imaging plates, and CT detector afterglow materials.

companies.name: Phosphor Technology (Tailored Lighting)
companies.use: Specialty phosphor manufacturer producing terbium-activated green phosphors for fluorescent lamps, X-ray screens, and specialty display applications. Supplies Tb-phosphors to lamp and screen manufacturers globally.

industries.name: Magnetostrictive Materials & Other
uses: Terfenol-D (Tb₀.₃Dy₀.₇Fe₂): the highest-performance magnetostrictive material — changes shape significantly (up to 2000 ppm strain) when exposed to a magnetic field. Used in sonar transducers, ultrasonic cleaners, active vibration control, and precision actuators
Terbium iron alloy (TbFe) as a component in magneto-optical recording media — TbFe and TbFeCo films used in magneto-optical disks (MO disks) popular in Japan for archival storage

Tb₃Ga₅O₁₂ (TGG, terbium gallium garnet) as Faraday rotator material in optical isolators for high-power lasers — TGG rotates the plane of polarization under a magnetic field, preventing back-reflections that could damage laser sources
Terbium activated GSO (Gd₂SiO₅:Tb) and YAG:Tb scintillators for electron microscopy detectors and synchrotron X-ray beamline imaging systems

companies.name: Etrema Products (TdVib)
companies.use: World's primary Terfenol-D manufacturer. Produces Terfenol-D rods and transducers for sonar arrays, ultrasonic cleaners, active vibration control in aerospace structures, and precision actuator applications requiring high-force, high-frequency actuation.
